Plan.path: Plan > Target Audience Analysis > Segmentation > Psychographic segmentation > Personality-trait models > Big Five / Five-Factor Model (FFM) > Openness to Experience > Emotional openness (feelings)

Prepare.path: Prepare > Develop Narratives > AI-agentic configuration space > Prompt configuration > Role confusion pressure narrative design

Execute.path: Execute > Drive Online Harms > AI-agentic architecture lattice > Retrieval memory pressure > Memory state class > Memory replay pressure within online harm generation
Execute.technique: Memory replay pressure within online harm generation

peer_exemplars.reasoning: This person's very low agreeableness, high libertarianism (98.9%), and high authoritarianism (75.98%) combine with the operation's platform outage narrative to produce a small update downward, as they already distrust transparency mandates as state overreach.

This person's high anti-elitism and moderate political trust combine with sustained exposure to a psychographically tailored campaign questioning institutional motives to produce a small reduction from 312, despite very low openness limiting engagement with complex transparency arguments.

The high openness, strong anti-domination and anti-censorship morals, combined with weakened institutional trust, allow the narrative of 'transparency as a cover for elite control' to modestly erode support, but the person's deep fairness and care values limit total reversal.

The person's very low agreeableness, high SDO, and strong anti-elitism make them skeptical of institutional transparency mandates, but their high openness and digital literacy limit the operation's ability to move them far from their baseline of 372.

Given very low openness, high authoritarian submission, and high system justification, the open-source discovery and avatar impersonation operation would be resisted as it targets a belief in transparency that is already low, so no movement.

High openness, high agreeableness, and trust in science/expertise (71%) make this person somewhat receptive to evidence-doubt narratives about transparency failures, but strong prior support limits movement to -20.

High openness and anti-authority views make the synthetic local resident narrative subtly erode support for transparency mandates by framing them as bureaucratic overreach, but baseline preference for transparency limits movement.

High agreeableness and trust in institutions make her resistant to the operation's narrative that transparency tools are weaponized by elites, but her high neuroticism and exposure to harassment create a small vulnerability to the pump-priming of municipal service disruption fears, yielding a modest 14-point drop.
